findings.evidence: `code/TauCeti/Algebra/Coalgebra/Comodule/TensorProduct.lean:58` defines only the raw map; nearby APIs package raw coactions once laws are proved, e.g. `code/TauCeti/Algebra/Coalgebra/Comodule/Product.lean:58` `prodCoact` and `:228` `def Prod : Comodule ...`; Mathlib supplies the target tensor coalgebra at `mathlib/Mathlib/RingTheory/Coalgebra/TensorProduct.lean:45` `instCoalgebraStruct` and `:130` `instCoalgebra`; the roadmap line `roadmap/TauCetiRoadmap/ReductiveGroups/README.md:128` asks for comodule tensor products.
findings.fix: Import the tensor coalgebra file and define a non-global packaged comodule, e.g. `def TensorProduct : Comodule R (C ⊗[R] D) (M ⊗[R] N)`, with `coact := externalTensorCoact`; keep the raw map and simp lemma as its coaction API.
findings.issue: `externalTensorCoact` exposes only `M ⊗ N →ₗ (M ⊗ N) ⊗ (C ⊗ D)`, leaving users without the natural `Comodule R (C ⊗[R] D) (M ⊗[R] N)` structure.

summary: The new declarations stop at the raw candidate map even though the natural API level for this roadmap item is the packaged tensor-product comodule over the tensor coalgebra.
